sourceAmbiguity: The blueprint says stale links after promotion or urgent diversion must not fall back to generic expired-link pages, but it does not name the exact bounded outcome.
decisionTaken: Promotion, urgent diversion, binding drift, route drift, and release drift supersede live continuation grants. Later redemption returns superseded_recovery or stale_link_recovery with a same-lineage RecoveryContinuationEnvelope.
whyThisFitsTheBlueprint: The result protects PHI-bearing seeded detail while keeping the caller in the same governed shell when recovery remains lawful.
operationalRisk: Without this distinction, a promoted or urgent case could either over-disclose via the old link or abandon the caller in a generic error page.
followUpIfPolicyChanges: Update 192_redemption_replay_and_supersession_cases.json and the service recovery reason mapping when promotion state classes expand.
